response.justification: Candidate is dramatically overqualified for an entry-level Junior Java role, with 15+ years of experience at senior/staff levels. Java is listed but is clearly not a primary language — the resume is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Most critically, German proficiency is self-rated as 'basic', which fails the explicit requirement of strong verbal and written German for client-facing insurance consulting work in Germany. Seniority mismatch, language gap, and lack of insurance/Java-focused experience make this a poor fit despite strong general engineering credentials.
strengths: Deep, broad software engineering background across web, mobile, and embedded systems
CS degree plus demonstrated agile/TDD/CI-CD practice aligns with methodology requirements
Already based in Berlin with EU work eligibility and prior client-facing consulting experience at Altom
concerns: German listed as 'basic' — fails the strong German communication requirement for insurance clients
Severely overqualified (Senior/Staff level) for an entry-level junior position; high attrition/comp-mismatch risk
Java appears only as a secondary skill; no demonstrated recent Java project work or insurance-sector exposure
